text: Refinement of H8's premise (CPU-only fragility sweep, e-0011): the Study-8 corpus is sampling-fragile only relative to a LOW-resolution / heuristic textual verifier, NOT in principle. Every continuous problem is robustly caught by a moderately denser uniform sampler (N_all: geom_01 22, geom_03 37, geom_04 20, geom_06 86, geom_10 17), and the two non-continuous problems fall to a dense integer scan (geom_05, prefix K=45) or any generic h-grid (geom_09, N=4) - the designer's naive grids (N=5-11) miss only because they are coarse. Consequence for the (still unrun) Study 8: forced_visual could only 'win' if forced_textual's sampler is held at the crippled low-N/sparse/decimal strategy; a forced_textual allowed ~100 uniform points would catch every falsifier. To make H8 a fair test of visual advantage rather than a baseline artifact, the forced_textual sampling strategy must be pinned and pre-registered. This does not change the pre-registered prediction but bounds its interpretation.
